intro: Basil the Great (c. 330–379), bishop of Caesarea in Cappadocia, was one of the three Cappadocian Fathers and a pillar of Nicene orthodoxy against Arianism. He wrote On the Holy Spirit and the Hexaemeron (homilies on the six days of creation), organised monastic life through his ascetic rules, and built one of the first great hospitals. He is a Doctor of the Church.
